packages.@carbon/react: Primary React component library (v11). Import components and icons from here.
packages.@carbon/web-components: Lit web components (v2.x, separate package & major). cds-* custom elements. For non-React.
packages.@carbon/ibm-products: Higher-level enterprise patterns (Tearsheet, PageHeader, SidePanel, advanced DataTable). Version-aligned with @carbon/react; some need feature flags.

components.name: Button
components.import: import { Button } from '@carbon/react'; import { Add } from '@carbon/react/icons';
components.summary: Action trigger. One primary per screen.

props.kind: primary | secondary | tertiary | ghost | danger | danger--tertiary | danger--ghost

rules: One primary per screen
Secondary only paired with a primary, never alone
Labels left-aligned, verb+noun ('Save changes'), sentence case, no truncation
Max 2-3 buttons per group (4+ -> menu/overflow)
Don't mix sizes in a group

components.name: TextInput
components.summary: Single-line text field. labelText and id are required for a11y.
rules: labelText and id required
Passing value requires onChange or the field is read-only
Labels: sentence case, brief, no colons
Mark (optional) on simple forms or (required) on enterprise forms, consistently
Fluid variant is 64px and stacks flush; don't mix default and fluid
components.example: <TextInput id="email" labelText="Email address" value={v} onChange={(e)=>set(e.target.value)} invalid={err} invalidText="Enter a valid email" />

components.name: Dropdown
components.summary: Single-select menu. Use radio buttons for <=2-3 options instead.

props.label: placeholder/trigger text
props.selectedItem+onChange: controlled; onChange returns { selectedItem } NOT a DOM event

rules: onChange returns { selectedItem }, not an event
Use radio buttons for 2-3 options
Always include a label (never placeholder-as-label)
Alphabetize options
Don't nest dropdowns
Related: MultiSelect (filter + select-all), ComboBox (select or type custom), FluidDropdown
components.example: <Dropdown id="dd" titleText="Choose an option" label="Select..." items={items} itemToString={(i)=>i?i.text:''} selectedItem={sel} onChange={({selectedItem})=>set(selectedItem)} />

components.name: Modal
components.import: import { Modal } from '@carbon/react'; // or ComposedModal + ModalHeader/ModalBody/ModalFooter
components.summary: Blocking dialog for critical moments only.
rules: Use ComposedModal for custom layouts
Types: passive, transactional, danger, acknowledgment, progress
Button placement automatic: secondary left, primary right
Use modals only for critical/blocking moments; non-critical -> toast or inline notification
components.example: Are you sure?

components.name: DataTable
components.import: import { DataTable, Table, TableHead, TableRow, TableHeader, TableBody, TableCell } from '@carbon/react';

props.rows: array with unique id per row

rules: Render-prop rows are TRANSFORMED (each has a .cells array) — not your raw rows
Spread getTableProps/getHeaderProps/getRowProps or sorting/selection/a11y break
Every raw row needs a unique id
5 row heights XS/S/M/L/XL — header row height must match body row height
Use skeleton loading (DataTableSkeleton), not spinners
Features: TableToolbar (<=5 actions + overflow), TableBatchActions on selection

components.name: Notification
components.summary: Status messaging. kind = info | success | warning | error.
rules: Inline: in task flow, persists until resolved
Toast: top-right, auto-dismiss ~5s
Actionable: adds a button
Callout: contextual, loads with page, never dismissible (guidance, not feedback)
Title has no trailing period; include next steps in errors
Never rely on color alone (status icon carries meaning)
components.example: <InlineNotification kind="error" title="Upload failed" subtitle="Check the file size and try again." />

components.name: Tabs
components.import: import { Tabs, TabList, Tab, TabPanels, TabPanel } from '@carbon/react';
components.summary: Switch between related views. One selected at a time.
rules: Prefer <=4 tabs (max 8 at large breakpoints, else scroll)
Align tab labels to the grid, not the tab container
Contained tabs attach to a panel on the same layer

components.name: Tile
components.import: import { Tile, ClickableTile, SelectableTile, ExpandableTile, TileGroup } from '@carbon/react';
components.summary: Flat content container. No drop shadows.

props.variants: base (read-only), ClickableTile (whole tile navigates), SelectableTile/TileGroup (single/multi-select), ExpandableTile

rules: Match variants within a group
No drop shadows — Carbon tiles are flat (border via feature flag, not shadow)
Separate surfaces with layer tokens

components.name: Accordion
components.summary: Collapsible sections; chevron end-aligned by default.
rules: Use to shorten pages / save space
Don't use for content users must read fully (use scrolling) or deep nesting (use TreeView)
Don't alternate icon placement on one page

components.name: Grid
rules: Column must be a direct child of Grid (or a nested subgrid)
CSS Grid is the v11 default; flexbox grid is opt-in ($use-flexbox-grid: true) with different classes
components.example: <Grid><Column sm={4} md={8} lg={16}>Full-width</Column><Column sm={4} md={4} lg={8}>Half</Column><Column sm={4} md={4} lg={8}>Half</Column></Grid>

components.name: UI Shell
components.import: import { Header, HeaderName, HeaderNavigation, HeaderMenuItem, HeaderGlobalBar, HeaderGlobalAction, SideNav, SideNavItems, SideNavLink, SkipToContent, Content } from '@carbon/react';
components.summary: App frame / global chrome. Use instead of a custom navbar.
rules: Use for global app chrome rather than a hand-rolled navbar
Pair with SkipToContent for a11y

components.name: Stack
components.summary: Vertical/horizontal spacing rhythm using spacing tokens.
rules: Prefer Stack gap over manual margins

components.name: Loading
components.import: import { Loading, InlineLoading, SkeletonText, SkeletonPlaceholder, DataTableSkeleton } from '@carbon/react';
components.summary: Loading states. Prefer skeletons over spinners for content placeholders.
rules: Prefer skeletons over spinners for content/table placeholders
Use InlineLoading for inline async status
